description: Vulnerability hidden inside a macro expansion. sprintf without bounds check wrapped in LOG_MSG macro. Mixed with safe snprintf findings.

sast_findings.message: sprintf(): Does not check for buffer overflows. Use snprintf() instead.
Buffer is accessed out of bounds.
snprintf(): On some very old systems, snprintf is incorrectly implemented.
The scope of the variable 'buf' can be reduced.

threat_context.title: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')

must_reject.reason: snprintf with sizeof - safe, and LOG_MSG_SAFE uses bounded version
variable scope is a style issue, not a vulnerability
